transcript.clean_text: the sun was shining on sienna this morning the host of the start of stage 12 of the giro d'italia a big day awaiting the riders the longest of the race so far at 212 kilometers and with a total of almost four and a half thousand meters of climbing an undulating first 75 cage would be the perfect opportunity for the early break to form but it was straight after that where the first of four classified climbs were located the last of those crested just inside 10 kilometers from the finish line located in banyo de la magna we had an early crash on the day and two prominent victims stage winner gino made of bahrain victorious and movie stars mark solaire who started 11th on gc both would get back on but both would eventually end up out of the race and so too would form a pink jersey where alessandro de marquis the crash leaving him with a broken collarbone and six broken ribs we had over 70 kilometers of attacking before break finally went clear today diego ulisse in a great position to add yet another giro state victory jeffrey bouchard also in there with the king of the mountains blue jersey and he over the course of the day would add 45 points to his tally in that competition the stage win was also up for grabs though and it was once again the breakaway that would take it andrea vendrami came close to stage win here two years ago and the italian from aj2r was on the offensive on the final climb of the day he would eventually have company george bennett of jumbo visma gianluca brambila of trek segafredo and chris hamilton of team dsm would join him but brambila and bennett were beginning to argue back in the bunch there was also some action an attack by chicony who was soon joined by his teammate vincenzo nebly it sent alarm bells ringing behind in york's grenadiers quick to chase it down up front the beef between brambila and bennett saw a gap open up in front of them and one which hamilton took full advantage of the australian accelerating clear and only bendrami was able to close it all that worked for over 210 k's only to watch the stage win fly out the window and up the road bendrami was forced to lead out he would kick with 150 meters to go and soon open up a two bike length gap between himself and the australian from that point onwards the win wasn't really in doubt a first at this year's race for ad2r a first in his career for andrea vendrami behind them there was more action on the descent the shark doing what he does best forcing the pace down it and wreaking havoc on those that tried to follow him in this instance it was gianni moscon he hit the deck would get back on albeit with a stuffing and a fair bit of pride knocked out of him ineos did still have riders to chase though and so although neebley did gain some time it would only be a handful of seconds all the rides in the top 10 on gc crossing the line safe and sound in the group of the pink jersey seven seconds after nebulae and so once again today the top 10 on the stage was filled by the riders from the breakaway behind bendrami and hamilton brambila and bennett were still arguing as they crossed the line in third and fourth respectively in fifth it was visconti and jeffrey bouchard happy with the point and happy with his teammate taking the win on the day no change at all in the top 10 on gc bernal still leads the way by 45 seconds over alexander vlassof but with solaire exiting the race today everybody just outside the top 10 has moved up a spot vincenzo neberly included tomorrow at stage 13 and one of the few remaining days for the sprinters they'll start near the adriatic coast in ravenna not far from the finish of stage five and then traced northwards eventually they will get to verona after 198 kilometers and there is not a single climb in sight and without much wind either this should be a relatively simple day until the customary chaotic closing kilometers you can catch as much of it as you want live over on gcm plus territory restrictions do apply though see you then you
